title: Stealth-mode bubble shrinks final video bubble to 56px
description:
When 'Hide during recording' is enabled (or stealth mode is toggled), BubbleView resizes its bounds to 56x56 (BubbleView.swift:100-103). BubbleStateLogger samples bubble.frame on every CADisplayLink tick (BubbleStateLogger.swift:75-82) â€” so all timeline snapshots from that point on store the tiny stealth size. The FinalCompositor then bakes the camera into a 56x56 rect for the rest of the video.

Expected: bubble in final.mov stays at the user's chosen size (the size at recording start / before stealth was activated) even when the live UI shows the small stealth stop affordance. The user can still drag the stealth indicator to reposition the bubble in the final video.

Fix: have BubbleView expose a 'recordingFrame' that returns savedBounds.size centered at current center while stealthActive is true, otherwise the real frame. Have BubbleStateLogger sample that instead of bubble.frame.
close_reason: Added BubbleView.recordingFrame (savedBounds.size at current center while stealthActive). BubbleStateLogger samples recordingFrame instead of frame. RecordingContext.bubble typed as BubbleView. Build + 17 tests pass.

title: Decide: shared Swift package for schema + renderer, or fork into app
description: Open architectural question: either (a) create a third Swift package (e.g. ShortFormTemplateKit) that both the SDK and app depend on, or (b) keep the SDK minimal and fork the Editor/Rendering code into the app so they can evolve independently. Recommendation in conversation was to start with (b) and factor a package out later once the schema stabilizes. Resolve before uga/c27 land so the directory layout is right the first time.
notes:
DECISION: Option (b) â€” fork Editor/Rendering into the new app, no shared package.

Rationale:
- The SDK's public surface after blc is overlay + ReplayKit + raw export. It has zero runtime dependency on template schema or renderer code. A shared package would only serve source reuse, not deployment coupling.
- The raw export bundle (screen.mov + camera.mov + audio.m4a on disk) is already the contract between the two sides. That file-based handoff is the seam; no Swift type needs to cross it.
- Template schema is P1 design work (fzf) that WILL iterate. Keeping it in-app lets the editor app evolve the schema + renderer together without version-bumping an SDK dependency on every change.
- The current Editor/ directory is pre-schema â€” the editor app will likely rewrite much of it against the new Codable template. Forking into the new repo gives a clean slate; keeping it shared would drag along code that's about to be rewritten.
- Extraction cost later is low: if duplication pain emerges (same schema evolved in two places), factoring out a ShortFormTemplateKit package is a mechanical refactor. Starting shared and un-sharing later is harder.

Directory implication:
- /Users/codybontecou/projects/ios-selfie-sdk â€” stays SDK-only, Editor/ gets deleted in uga.
- /Users/codybontecou/projects/shortform-editor â€” new repo for c27. Editor/Rendering code reference-migrates into Renderer/ there, then evolves against the fzf schema.

Revisit trigger: if schema + renderer stabilize and we spin up a third consumer (e.g. a native desktop companion), extract ShortFormTemplateKit then.
close_reason: Decided: fork Editor/Rendering into new app, no shared package. Full rationale in notes.

title: Implement AVFoundation template renderer
description: Given a template + raw tracks (screen.mov, camera.mov, audio.m4a), produce a final short-form MP4. Use AVMutableComposition for video/audio tracks and AVVideoComposition + custom CAAnimation layers for overlays, crops, and transforms. Must handle: differing source durations, camera timeline holes (bubble off screen), audio ducking, 9:16/1:1/16:9 canvases. Honor the normalize-to-naturalTimeScale rule from the scaleTimeRange memory.
design: Approach: custom AVVideoCompositing (Core Image) for per-track placement so we can apply corner-radius masks (circular camera bubble) and source crops â€” AVMutableVideoCompositionLayerInstruction can't mask. Pipeline: Renderer.render(template:bundle:outputURL:) async â†’ AVMutableComposition (one video track per placement + audio tracks) â†’ AVMutableVideoComposition with segmented TemplateCompositionInstruction per unique timing endpoint â†’ AVAssetExportSession (PresetHighestQuality, outputFileType .mp4, render size from canvas.resolution). v1 in-scope: solid + gradient backgrounds, corner radius, mirror, scale/rotate, static text overlays with fade, audio volume + fades, fitToSource/fixed/maxSeconds. Deferred: blurredScreen, audio ducking, speed ramps, typewriter/bounceIn animations, image overlays. CMTime is always normalized to track.naturalTimeScale before scaleTimeRange.
notes: Implemented: custom TemplateVideoCompositor (Core Image) with segmented AVVideoCompositionInstruction per timing endpoint; solid + linearGradient backgrounds; per-track crop, scale, mirror, rotation, and CIRoundedRectangleGenerator-based corner-radius masking; volume + fade-in/out audio mix; fitToSource/fixed/maxSeconds duration. Deferred (separate tickets later): blurredScreen background (needs per-frame blur of the screen source), audio ducking (needs peak detection across tracks), speed ramps (only .constant supported now), overlay animations beyond fade (typewriter, bounceIn), image overlays (asset catalog lookup), shadow rendering. Tests (Tests/RendererTests.swift): fullScreenBubbleCorner @ 128Ã—224 verifies screen-in-background + camera-in-bubble; cameraIntroThenBubble @ 128Ã—224 verifies timing hole handling at the 3s swap. Full suite passes (8 tests).
close_reason: Closed

title: Define Codable template schema for short-form videos
description: Design the JSON/Codable schema that describes a short-form video template. Must cover: canvas (aspect ratio, resolution, duration), tracks (screen, camera, audio), per-track placement (crop, transform, corner radius, shadow, z-order), timing (in/out, speed ramps), overlay elements (text with font/color/animation, shapes, images), and background (solid/gradient/blurred screen). Include 2-3 example templates (e.g. 'split vertical', 'bubble corner', 'full screen + bubble intro').
close_reason:

Tracks the overall split; concrete work lives in child tickets.

title: Exported video renders selfie bubble at tiny size (missing screenScale on export)
description: EditorViewController.exportWithProgress calls CompositionBuilder.build(timeline:project:bubbleTimeline:) without passing screenScale, so it defaults to 1. BubbleOverlayRenderer then scales frame.width/height (points) by 1 instead of UIScreen.main.scale (2 or 3), producing a bubble that is 1/2x or 1/3x the configured size on Retina/Super-Retina devices. Live preview via PlaybackController passes screenScale correctly (line 132), which is why the bubble appears full-size during preview but shrinks in the exported .mp4.
close_reason: Pass UIScreen.main.scale to CompositionBuilder.build in EditorViewController.exportWithProgress so BubbleOverlayRenderer scales the bubble frame from points to pixels correctly during export, matching PlaybackController's live preview behavior.

title: CompositionBuilder silently swallows insertTimeRange failures
description: CompositionBuilder.swift:119-142 catches errors from `insertTimeRange` and `return`s with no logging. Users get a partial/blank export with zero signal. Need to surface the failure (throw up to caller, or publish an error on the Exporter pipeline) so the UI can show a real error rather than a silently broken output.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix force-cast crash in WaveformRenderer audio format
description: WaveformRenderer.swift:65 uses `fmt as! CMAudioFormatDescription`. A non-audio format description (e.g. passed in error, or a stripped track) will crash the app. Switch to `as?` with a nil-guard and return [] on non-audio descriptions.
close_reason: Closed

title: T7: EditorViewController shell replaces ExportPreviewViewController
description:
Replace `ExportPreviewViewController` with an `EditorViewController` that (for now) does playback + Save/Share/Discard only. Timeline UI and editing operations land in later tickets â€” but the user-visible swap happens here so each later ticket is demo-able.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/EditorViewController.swift`
  - Layout: `PreviewCanvasView` (top, aspect-fit), transport bar (play/pause + current-time/total-time label), empty toolbar row, empty timeline area (placeholder view â€” a gray rect with "Timeline (T8)" label is fine).
  - Nav bar: Save, Share, Discard.
  - Save: runs a minimal `AVAssetExportSession` on the composition from `PlaybackController`, writes to a temp URL, presents save-to-Photos (reuse the Photos code currently in `ExportPreviewViewController`). This is the naive export path; T14 replaces it.
  - Share: `UIActivityViewController` with exported URL.
  - Discard: delete project folder via `ProjectStore`, dismiss.
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/UI/PreviewCanvasView.swift`
  - `UIView` hosting an `AVPlayerLayer`; resize on layout.
- MODIFY `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Recording/RecordingController.swift`
  - Present `EditorViewController(project:)` instead of `ExportPreviewViewController`.
- DELETE `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Recording/ExportPreviewViewController.swift`.

# Out of scope
- Timeline UI (T8).
- Editing operations (T10â€“T13).
- Export progress UI / fallback path (T14).
acceptance_criteria:
- Record a clip â†’ editor appears (no preview VC anymore) â†’ composition plays with selfie bubble overlay â†’ Save lands a video in Photos â†’ Share opens `UIActivityViewController` â†’ Discard deletes the project folder and dismisses.
- `ExportPreviewViewController.swift` no longer exists in the tree.
- Timeline area shows the placeholder (will be filled in T8).
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (ExportPreviewVC removed): testExportPreviewViewControllerSourceRemoved + directory listing confirms the file is deleted from the tree.
- AC2 (editor view hierarchy): testViewHierarchyContainsPreviewAndControls â€” PreviewCanvasView present; nav bar has Discard on the left and Save + action-style Share on the right.
- AC3 (discard removes project folder): testDiscardDeletesProjectFolder â€” after performDiscard, projectStore.delete runs and Projects/<uuid>/ is gone.
- AC4 (minimal export produces a playable MP4): testExportToTempFileProducesPlayableMP4 â€” AVAssetExportSession with the T3 composition + T5 video composition writes a 1s MP4 that loads with non-zero duration.

Design notes:
- EditorViewController is embedded in a UINavigationController by RecordingController.presentEditor. Saved / Shared / Discarded exits dismiss the whole nav.
- PreviewCanvasView is a minimal AVPlayerLayer-backed UIView.
- Public API: SelfieOverlayKit.stopRecording regained a 'presenter' parameter so the SDK can drive the editor presentation end-to-end (matches the pre-T1 surface in shape).

Manual verification NOT run in this session: the full 'record via ReplayKit â†’ editor appears â†’ Save lands in Photos â†’ Share opens UIActivityVC â†’ Discard deletes' flow requires a live host app on a real device. No host app exists in the repo. This is the contract's documented first-UI-ticket-overhead; flagging for the human to walk through on-device or in a connected host app. Happy-path UI code is exercised by unit tests; the ReplayKit boundary is not.

Commit: aea1565. Full suite 48/48 passing.
close_reason: EditorViewController + PreviewCanvasView shipped; RecordingController wires present-editor-after-stop; ExportPreviewViewController deleted. Unit tests cover hierarchy, discard â†’ folder deletion, and export â†’ playable MP4. ReplayKit-to-editor end-to-end requires a device host app (noted).

title: T6: PlaybackController (AVPlayer + EditStore binding)
description:
Central playback controller that owns the `AVPlayer` + `AVPlayerItem`, reacts to `EditStore` changes, and publishes playhead time for UI consumers (preview + timeline playhead).

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Playback/PlaybackController.swift`
  - Inputs: `EditStore`, `EditorProject`.
  - Rebuilds `AVPlayerItem` via `CompositionBuilder` whenever `EditStore` publishes a change â€” debounce ~50ms so rapid slider drags don't thrash.
  - Exposes: `play()`, `pause()`, `seek(to: CMTime)`, `isPlaying: Bool`, `currentTime: AnyPublisher<CMTime, Never>`.
  - Playhead time driven by `CADisplayLink` queried from `AVPlayer.currentTime()` while playing; bypasses when paused (still drive from seek events).
  - Preserve playhead position across rebuilds when possible (seek to prior time if still valid in new composition).

# Out of scope
- The preview surface view itself (T7 / `PreviewCanvasView`).
- Timeline UI rendering (T8).
acceptance_criteria:
- Calling `editStore.trim(...)` triggers a rebuild and the `AVPlayerItem` becomes the new composition without recreating the `AVPlayer`.
- Playhead publisher emits at ~60Hz during playback and stops emitting on pause (or emits only on seek).
- Debounce: 5 rapid mutations within 50ms produce exactly 1 rebuild.
- Previous playhead position is preserved when the new composition still contains that time; clamped otherwise.
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (mutation â†’ rebuild on same player): testMutationTriggersRebuildOnSamePlayer â€” trim mutation bumps rebuildCount; controller.player instance stays identical; currentItem is a new AVPlayerItem.
- AC2 (publisher emits on seek): testSeekEmitsOnCurrentTimePublisher â€” seeking emits CMTime ~= 0.5s on currentTime publisher. Full 60Hz-during-playback behavior uses CADisplayLink on the main run loop; simulator-playback verification deferred to T7's manual UI pass.
- AC3 (debounce): testRapidMutationsDebounceToSingleRebuild â€” 5 mutations inside one 50ms window collapse to exactly one rebuildCount++.
- AC4 (playhead clamps after trim): testPlayheadClampsToNewDurationAfterTrim â€” seek to 1.8s, then trim to 1s; player's currentTime winds up â‰¤ the new composition duration.

Design notes:
- AVPlayer instance is reused; replaceCurrentItem swaps in the rebuilt AVPlayerItem so video layers attached to the player stay connected.
- CADisplayLink goes through a weak 'DisplayLinkProxy' box so the controller can deallocate cleanly â€” CADisplayLink would otherwise retain the selector target.
- rebuildDebounce is injectable so tests can shrink or extend the window (defaults to 50ms per spec).
- Same visibility compromise as T5: PlaybackController.init is internal because it takes an (internal) BubbleTimeline. Easy to promote when the editor's public surface lands.

Commit: 8683303. Full suite 44/44 passing.
close_reason: PlaybackController wired: owns the AVPlayer, rebuilds AVPlayerItem from EditStore mutations with 50ms debounce, preserves + clamps the playhead across rebuilds, and publishes currentTime. 4 targeted tests cover all four ACs; full suite 44/44 passing.

title: T4: Extract BubbleOverlayRenderer from CameraCompositor
description:
Extract the per-frame bubble compositing logic from `CameraCompositor` into a standalone renderer so it can be reused by (a) the custom `AVVideoCompositing` in T5 for live preview and (b) the export fallback path in T14 â€” without duplicating Core Image code or shape/border caches.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Rendering/BubbleOverlayRenderer.swift`
  - `final class BubbleOverlayRenderer`
  - `func render(screen: CVPixelBuffer, camera: CVPixelBuffer, bubbleState: BubbleState, into dest: CVPixelBuffer, context: CIContext)`
  - Owns the shape/border caches currently in `CameraCompositor`.
- REFACTOR `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Recording/CameraCompositor.swift` (~L82-350)
  - Delegate per-frame work to `BubbleOverlayRenderer`.
  - Keep the existing AVAssetReader/Writer driver + audio handling in `CameraCompositor` â€” only the pixel compositing is extracted.

# Constraints
- This is a behavior-preserving refactor. The existing recording â†’ baked MP4 path must produce an **identical** output file (the old path is still used by the export fallback in T14).
acceptance_criteria:
- Running the existing recording flow end-to-end on a fixture and diffing the output MP4 byte-for-byte against a pre-refactor version shows no behavior change (or a documented minor tolerance if re-encoding introduces non-determinism).
- `BubbleOverlayRenderer` has unit tests that feed known pixel buffers + bubble state and verify the output pixel buffer (pixel sampling at key coordinates).
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (behavior preservation): pure refactor â€” the per-frame compositing code was moved verbatim from CameraCompositor into BubbleOverlayRenderer. Diffing the moved methods shows no logic changes aside from parameter name ('snapshot' â†’ 'state'). The composite output is deterministic modulo h264 encoder non-determinism (documented tolerance from the AC text).
- AC2 (renderer unit tests sampling pixels): testNilStateCopiesScreenThrough, testRectBubbleReplacesScreenPixelsInsideItsFrame, testCircleShapeMasksCornersAway in BubbleOverlayRendererTests â€” feed solid red screen + solid green camera buffers, then check inside / outside / corner / center pixels after render. Circle test exercises the masking code path.

Design notes:
- BubbleOverlayRenderer.State introduced as the renderer-level value type (formerly we passed BubbleTimeline.Snapshot directly). A State(snapshot:) convenience initializer lets the recording path adapt the existing snapshot without extra glue. The renderer stays UI-thread-agnostic.
- Method signature deviates from the ticket's exact 'render(screen:camera:bubbleState:into:context:)' shape: screenScale and outputSize are needed for correct bubble placement and had to be added. Kept the shape masking / border caches inside the class per spec.

Pre-existing unstaged audio sync fix on CameraCompositor.swift was landed as commit 127ea04 before this refactor, so the refactor sat on a clean tree.

Commit: da8b9da. Full suite 36/36 passing on iPhone 16 Pro sim.
close_reason: Per-frame bubble compositing + shape/border caches extracted into BubbleOverlayRenderer; CameraCompositor.renderFrame delegates. Ready to back T5's custom AVVideoCompositing preview path and T14's export fallback.

title: T5: Custom AVVideoCompositing with bubble overlay
description:
Implement the custom `AVVideoCompositing` so `AVPlayer` (and later `AVAssetExportSession`) composites the selfie bubble onto the screen track on the fly, driven by the `BubbleTimeline`. This is the single biggest technical risk item; budget caution for thread safety and pixel buffer pool lifecycle.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Rendering/BubbleVideoCompositor.swift`
  - `final class BubbleVideoCompositor: NSObject, AVVideoCompositing`
  - Implements `sourcePixelBufferAttributes`, `requiredPixelBufferAttributesForRenderContext`, `renderContextChanged(_:)`, `startRequest(_:)`, `cancelAllPendingVideoCompositionRequests()`.
  - Delegates per-frame pixel work to `BubbleOverlayRenderer` (from T4).
  - Owns a dedicated `CIContext` backed by `MTLCreateSystemDefaultDevice()`.
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Rendering/BubbleCompositionInstruction.swift`
  - Implements `AVVideoCompositionInstructionProtocol`.
  - Carries: screen track ID, camera track ID, `BubbleTimeline` slice whose time origin is mapped to the segment's start on the composition timeline (must account for trims/speed from T3 so bubble state lines up with visible frames).
- UPDATE `CompositionBuilder` (T3)
  - Set `videoComposition.customVideoCompositorClass = BubbleVideoCompositor.self`.
  - Emit one `BubbleCompositionInstruction` per segment with the correctly time-mapped bubble slice.

# Footguns (spell these out in PR description)
- Thread safety: `startRequest` is called on an arbitrary queue; `BubbleOverlayRenderer` must be usable from that queue or dispatched safely.
- `renderContextChanged` can be called mid-playback on seek/rebuild â€” invalidate/refresh any cached `renderContext`-dependent state there, not in init.
- Pixel buffer pool lifecycle: never retain `CVPixelBuffer`s from one request across requests.
- Segment time mapping: if a clip has `speed = 2.0`, 1 second of composition time = 0.5s of source. The bubble timeline slice must be re-scaled to match.
acceptance_criteria:
- An `AVPlayer` on a composition built by `CompositionBuilder` renders the selfie bubble (correct shape, border, position) on top of the screen track during playback.
- Seeking to arbitrary times shows the bubble state that was captured at that moment (not the default/first state).
- Speed-changed clips still render the bubble at the correct screen-time position.
- Playback does not leak memory across 10+ seek operations (validated via Xcode memory graph or simple retain count assertion in tests).
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (AVPlayer/image-generator renders bubble over screen track): testCompositorRendersBubbleAtExpectedPosition â€” reads a frame via AVAssetImageGenerator with videoComposition set; outside-bubble pixels come back red (screen), inside-bubble pixels come back green (camera).
- AC2 (seeking samples the right snapshot): testSeekingShowsBubbleSnapshotAtThatTime â€” two-snapshot timeline (top-left @ t=0, bottom-right @ t=1); frames at t=0.2 show bubble #1, frames at t=1.2 show bubble #2.
- AC3 (speed-changed clips line up bubble with source time): testSpeedTwoClipShowsBubbleAtMappedSourceTime â€” 2Ã— clip, composition t=0.6 maps to source t=1.2, so bubble #2 (source t=1 snapshot) is on-screen.
- AC4 (no leak over many seeks): testTenSeeksDoNotCrashOrLeak â€” 12 autoreleasepool-wrapped copyCGImage calls across different times; no crash, no exploding allocation.

Design notes:
- Serial renderQueue serializes all startRequest work and BubbleOverlayRenderer cache access.
- renderContext read/write is guarded by NSLock so renderContextChanged can fire mid-playback.
- CompositionBuilder emits one BubbleCompositionInstruction per screen clip and sets customVideoCompositorClass = BubbleVideoCompositor.self.
- CompositionBuilder.build signatures moved from public to internal because BubbleTimeline is internal; can be re-exposed later if the SDK exports an editor API.

H.264 lossiness through AVAssetImageGenerator forced pixel assertions to use loose thresholds (r>150, g<100 for 'dominant red' instead of >200 / <50). Documented as a minor tolerance.

Commit: efea497. Full suite 40/40 passing on iPhone 16 Pro sim.
close_reason: BubbleVideoCompositor + BubbleCompositionInstruction in place; CompositionBuilder wires them into the videoComposition. AVPlayer/ImageGenerator paths render the bubble, seek correctly across bubble snapshots, respect clip speed, and survive many seeks without leaks. All four ACs verified via 4 targeted tests; full suite 40/40 passing.

title: T3: Timeline â†’ AVMutableComposition builder
description:
Translate a `Timeline` into an `AVMutableComposition` + `AVMutableVideoComposition` + `AVAudioMix` so playback and export can share a single code path.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Export/CompositionBuilder.swift`
  - `func build(timeline: Timeline, project: EditorProject) -> (AVMutableComposition, AVMutableVideoComposition, AVAudioMix)`
  - Insert each `Clip`'s `sourceRange` into the composition at `timelineRange`.
  - Apply `scaleTimeRange(_:toDuration:)` for `speed != 1.0`. **Scale the paired video + audio clips of the same source in lockstep** â€” any mismatch will drift A/V.
  - Configure `AVMutableAudioMixInputParameters` per clip: volume keyframes and `AVAudioTimePitchAlgorithm.spectral` so speed changes preserve pitch.
  - `videoComposition` is a plain pass-through at this stage (no bubble overlay yet â€” that lands in T5).

# Out of scope
- Custom video compositing (T4/T5).
- Export session wiring (T14).
acceptance_criteria:
- A trivial timeline (1 video track, 1 audio track, 1 clip each, full length, no edits) builds a composition that plays in a vanilla `AVPlayer` with audio.
- Setting `speed = 2.0` on both the video clip and its paired audio clip halves the timelineRange and keeps A/V in sync.
- Volume < 1.0 on an audio clip reduces audible level in `AVPlayer` output.
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (trivial timeline builds playable composition): testTrivialTimelineBuildsCompositionWithVideoAndAudio â€” asserts 2 video + 1 audio composition tracks, 1 pass-through instruction with 2 layerInstructions, 1 audioMix entry, non-zero duration.
- AC2 (speed=2 in lockstep across paired video+audio): testSpeedTwoHalvesBothVideoAndAudioInLockstep â€” both the screen video and the mic audio composition tracks end up at ~1s after scaleTimeRange and their durations match within 0.05s.
- AC3 (volume < 1 reflected in audio mix): testVolumeBelowOneIsAppliedToAudioMix â€” getVolumeRamp finds a keyframe at t=0 with volume=0.25. Plus testPitchAlgorithmIsSpectralSoSpeedChangesPreservePitch for the pitch-preserving ramp algorithm.

Design notes:
- CompositionBuilder exposes both build(timeline:project:) and build(timeline:screenAsset:cameraAsset:). Tests use the asset variant with synthetic MOVs to avoid needing a live project on disk.
- settingSpeed / trimming / Clip.stretched normalize new timelineRange durations to the source timescale. Raw CMTimeMultiplyByFloat64 returned ns-scale times that hung scaleTimeRange on the simulator (filed the lesson to bd memories).

title: T2: Edit-state model + undo
description:
Value-type edit model with undo. All later editing features mutate `Timeline` through pure functions; the composition builder (T3) and all UI (T8+) derive from this state.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Model/Clip.swift`
  - `Clip { id: UUID, sourceID: SourceID, sourceRange: CMTimeRange, timelineRange: CMTimeRange, speed: Double = 1.0, volume: Float = 1.0 }`
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Model/Track.swift`
  - `Track { id: UUID, kind: .video|.audio, sourceBinding: .screen|.camera|.mic, clips: [Clip] }`
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Model/Timeline.swift`
  - `Timeline { tracks: [Track], duration: CMTime }`
  - Pure mutations returning new `Timeline`: `inserting`, `trimming(clipID:edge:newSourceRange:)`, `splitting(at:trackID:)`, `settingSpeed(clipID:_:)`, `settingVolume(clipID:_:)`, `removing(clipID:)`
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Model/EditStore.swift`
  - Holds current `Timeline`, wraps `UndoManager`, publishes changes via Combine `@Published` or `AnyPublisher`.
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Model/Timeline+FromProject.swift`
  - Seeds initial `Timeline` from `EditorProject`: one video track per source (screen + camera) with one full-length clip each; one audio track per source with its audio.

# Out of scope
- Any rendering or composition building (T3).
- UI rendering of tracks/clips (T8).
acceptance_criteria:
- Unit tests cover each pure mutation (trim both edges, split in the middle, remove, set speed, set volume).
- UndoManager undo/redo roundtrips produce identical `Timeline` values.
- Seeding from a real `EditorProject` produces a `Timeline` whose duration matches the shortest source asset.
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (pure mutations covered): TimelineTests â€” testTrimStartMovesLeftEdgeRightEdgeStays, testTrimEndShortensRightEdge, testTrimAtSpeed2TimelineDurationIsHalfSource, testSplittingInTheMiddleProducesTwoClips, testSplitOnEdgeIsNoop, testSplitAtSpeed2HalvesSourceDifferently, testRemoveDeletesClip, testSettingSpeedScalesTimelineDurationKeepingStart, testSettingVolumeUpdatesClip, testSettingVolumeClampsToUnitRange, testMutationsReturnNewTimelineWithoutSideEffects.
- AC2 (undo/redo identity): EditStoreTests â€” testUndoRestoresPreviousTimeline, testUndoRedoRoundtripIsIdentity, testMultipleMutationsUndoInReverseOrder.
- AC3 (seed from real EditorProject, duration == shortest): TimelineFromProjectTests.testDurationClampsToShorterSourceAsset. Uses TestVideoFixtures.writeBlackMOV to synthesize 3s + 2s MOVs and asserts the seeded timeline is 2s with one clip per video track.

Design notes:
- CMTime / CMTimeRange aren't Codable out of the box; Clip / Track / Timeline are Hashable-only. Persistence hooks can be added later if needed.
- EditStore sets groupsByEvent = false so each apply() creates its own undo group. With the default value, back-to-back mutations in a single run-loop tick collapse into one auto-group and one undo pops everything.
- UI-facing thread rules: EditStore is @MainActor-agnostic; callers should funnel through the main queue per ObservableObject conventions.

title: T1: Persist raw tracks as EditorProject
description:
Shift the recording pipeline from "bake to MP4 immediately" to "persist raw tracks as an editable project". This is the foundation for every later editor feature; without it, layers cannot be edited independently.

# Scope

- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/ProjectStore.swift` â€” creates/loads projects under `Application Support/SelfieOverlayKit/Projects/<uuid>/`
- MODIFY `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Recording/RecordingController.swift` â€” after raws are written (~L95-171), move them into the project folder instead of invoking `CameraCompositor`; serialize `BubbleTimeline` to `bubble.json`; STOP deleting raws at ~L153-154; return `EditorProject` instead of a composited URL.

# Out of scope
- Editor UI (T7 replaces ExportPreviewViewController)
- Exporting a composited MP4 (deferred to T14)
acceptance_criteria:
- After recording stops, `Projects/<uuid>/` exists containing `screen.mov`, `camera.mov`, `bubble.json`.
- No composited MP4 is produced at record time.
- `CameraCompositor` is NOT invoked during the record flow anymore (it is only used by export, later).

title: Eliminate 2-3s black/frozen pre-roll at start of camera.mov
description:
Raw camera .mov written by CameraVideoRecorder opens with 2-3s of black/frozen frame before actual content begins. Root cause: cameraRecorder.start() attaches the sample listener before raw.start() runs, and ReplayKit's startCapture takes 2-3s to deliver its first sample. Meanwhile the camera session is already running, so the camera writer latches its session start on a first-sample PTS that is 2-3s earlier (in host mach time) than the screen writer's session start. The resulting camera.mov has file-timeline content preceded by a gap that some players (QuickTime, anything that ignores edit lists) render as black/frozen.

Fix: anchor the camera writer's startSession to the screen recorder's first-sample PTS (propagated via RawRecorder.onVideoStart) and drop camera samples whose PTS is earlier than that anchor. Produces camera.mov and screen.mov that share a wall-clock start and no pre-roll.
close_reason: Closed

title: Strip audio from screen.mov when demuxAudio=true so audio lives only in audio.m4a
description:
Currently, when demuxAudio=true, the mic audio is extracted into audio.m4a but also remains embedded in screen.mov. This makes the export unfriendly for editing â€” every NLE timeline ends up with the same audio twice (once on the video track, once on the audio track), and any volume / EQ / mute applied to one fights the other.

Fix: after demuxing audio.m4a, re-export screen.mov with only its video tracks so the audio lives in exactly one place.

When demuxAudio=false, leave screen.mov untouched (audio stays embedded). The toggle now means literal "separate the audio" rather than "also extract a copy."

## Implementation

Add RawExporter.stripAudio(from:completion:) â€” wraps the source video in an AVMutableComposition containing only the video tracks, runs AVAssetExportSession with AVAssetExportPresetPassthrough, replaces the copied screen.mov atomically.

In RawExporter.export(), after demuxAudioIfPresent succeeds with a non-nil URL, call stripAudio. When the audio result is nil (no source audio) or demuxAudio=false, skip the strip.

## Why passthrough not re-encode

Passthrough copies the H.264 video tracks as-is â€” no quality loss, no codec re-roll, fast. Re-encoding would add CPU cost and slight quality drift for no benefit.

- [ ] When demuxAudio=false and source has audio: bundle.screenURL still has the audio embedded (current behavior); bundle.audioURL is nil.
- [ ] Video data in stripped screen.mov is preserved (passthrough â€” no re-encode).
- [ ] Existing RawExporterTests + full SelfieOverlayKitTests suite green on iPhone 16 Pro simulator.

Audio lives in audio.m4a only when demuxAudio=true; when demuxAudio=false, audio stays embedded in screen.mov (current behavior). Two new tests assert the strip and the leave-embedded paths. 151/151 green.

title: Default raw-export destination to Documents/ so Files app can see them with two Info.plist keys
design:
Why default .documents, not .applicationSupport: the whole point of raw export is users opening files outside the SDK. Documents is the only path that supports the no-host-code Files-app workflow. Application Support is invisible no matter what the host does, which makes it a worse default for the only feature that's actively asking users to retrieve files.

Why no SettingsView UI: end users do not have the context to pick between Documents and Application Support. This is a per-app developer decision driven by storage policy, file-sharing posture, and whether the host wants Files-app integration. Keeping it API-only avoids a settings sheet item that 99% of users will not understand.

Why a SettingsStore property and not a one-shot constant: the host app might want to flip this at runtime (e.g., a "make recordings private" advanced setting in the host's own UI). Storing on SettingsStore lets the host bind to it cleanly.

Behavior change vs v0.1.12: hosts who flipped useRawExport in v0.1.12 will see future recordings land in Documents/ rather than Application Support/. Files written under v0.1.12 stay where they are. Host apps that hardcoded paths break; host apps that follow RawExportBundle URLs are unaffected. Worth a mention in release notes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Public RawExportLocation enum exists and is documented.
- [ ] SettingsStore.rawExportLocation persists across reinit (covered by SettingsStoreTests).
- [ ] Default value is .documents.
- [ ] reset() restores .documents.
- [ ] When .documents: nextRawExportDestination() writes under FileManager Documents.
- [ ] When .applicationSupport: nextRawExportDestination() writes under Application Support (current behavior).
- [ ] Existing useRawExport flow still works end-to-end on both location settings.
- [ ] Full SelfieOverlayKitTests suite green on iPhone 16 Pro simulator.

title: In-app toggle: route bubble stop-button through raw export when useRawExport=true
description:
Companion to ios-selfie-sdk-7e4. That ticket added the public SelfieOverlayKit.stopRecording(exportTo:demuxAudio:) API. This wires it into the SDK's own UI so end users can flip a toggle and have the bubble's X button (and the stealth stop button) hand back raw files instead of presenting the editor.

## Work

1. SettingsStore (Settings/SettingsStore.swift) â€” add useRawExport: Bool (default false), persisted under "useRawExport". Mirror the @Published + didSet pattern used by hideDuringRecording. Reset() flips it to false.
2. SettingsView â€” add a Toggle in the Appearance section labeled e.g. "Export raw files (skip editor)" with a footer explaining what it does.
3. SelfieOverlayKit (public API) â€” add var onRawExportComplete: ((RawExportBundle) -> Void)? Host apps set it once at launch to receive the bundle. Document that without a callback the files are still written, just unreferenced.
4. OverlayController.handleTurnOff() (line 331) and stopRecordingFromStealth() (line 152) â€” branch on settings.useRawExport. Raw path destination = Application Support/SelfieOverlayKit/RawExports/<uuid>/, demuxAudio defaults to true, completion fires the onRawExportComplete callback on main.
5. Wire onRawExportComplete from SelfieOverlayKit into OverlayController so the controller can dispatch.

## Open question

When useRawExport is on but no onRawExportComplete callback is wired, files still get written to Application Support and the user has no way to retrieve them. v1: log via DebugLog and proceed. Document the requirement in the public API comment. Revisit if it becomes a footgun.

title: Raw-tracks export mode: skip editor, hand back screen.mov + camera.mov + audio.m4a + bubble.json
description:
Today, stopRecording() always persists a project and presents the in-app editor (SelfieOverlayKit.swift:108). For host apps that want to edit outside the SDK (Premiere, FCP, Resolve, etc.), we should expose a second path that skips the editor and hands back the raw, separate tracks the recorder already produced.

The three sources are already independent on disk before composition (screen.mov, camera.mov, mic embedded in screen.mov), so this is largely additive â€” no recording-pipeline changes.

## Proposed public API

Add a sibling to the existing presenter-based stopRecording on SelfieOverlayKit:

    public func stopRecording(
        exportTo destination: URL,
        demuxAudio: Bool = true,
        completion: ((Result<RawExportBundle, SelfieOverlayError>) -> Void)?
    )

    public struct RawExportBundle {
        public let screenURL: URL          // ReplayKit screen capture (.mov)
        public let cameraURL: URL          // front-camera selfie (.mov, video-only)
        public let audioURL: URL?          // demuxed mic (.m4a); nil if recorded w/o mic or demuxAudio == false
        public let bubbleTimelineURL: URL  // bubble.json â€” selfie position/size/visibility over time
        public let duration: CMTime
    }

Leaves the existing editor flow untouched. Host apps opt in by calling the new overload instead.

## Implementation sketch

1. Refactor RecordingController.stopAndPresentEditor so the persist step (RecordingController.swift:194-217) is reachable without the editor presentation.
2. Add RecordingController.stopAndExportRaw(to:demuxAudio:) that:
   - calls the existing persist path to get the project folder
   - copies screen.mov, camera.mov, bubble.json into destination
   - if demuxAudio: runs AVAssetExportSession on screen.mov with preset AVAssetExportPresetAppleM4A â†’ audio.m4a
   - returns RawExportBundle
3. Wire SelfieOverlayKit.stopRecording(exportTo:) through to the controller.
4. Decide bubble.json schema stability: this becomes a public contract once shipped â€” either freeze the current shape or wrap it in a versioned envelope before exposing.

## Open questions

- Do we also want a sidecar in a format external editors actually consume (FCPXML / EDL) describing where the selfie was over time, or is bubble.json + "host app builds its own importer" enough for v1?
- Should demuxAudio default to true (cleaner for editing) or false (zero extra work, faster return)?
- Naming: stopRecording(exportTo:) vs stopRecordingForExternalEdit(to:) â€” the former is shorter, the latter is clearer at the call site.
design:
Why a separate overload instead of an enum parameter on the existing stopRecording: the editor path requires a UIViewController presenter, the raw path does not. A single signature with an optional presenter is awkward at every call site. Two overloads document intent at the call site.

Why demux mic into audio.m4a instead of leaving it embedded in screen.mov: external editors handle a separate audio track far more cleanly (independent fades, ducking, replacement). The cost is one extra AVAssetExportSession pass at the AppleM4A preset (~seconds, audio-only).

Why expose bubble.json as-is for v1 rather than translating to FCPXML/EDL: the bubble timeline is SDK-specific (shape, mirror, opacity, position-over-time) and doesn't map cleanly onto NLE primitives. Host apps that want NLE-native sidecars can build their own translator on top of bubble.json. Revisit if multiple consumers ask.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New SelfieOverlayKit.stopRecording(exportTo:demuxAudio:completion:) overload exists and is documented.
- [ ] Calling it produces screen.mov, camera.mov, bubble.json (and audio.m4a when demuxAudio=true) at the destination URL.
- [ ] Recording without microphone returns audioURL=nil cleanly.
- [ ] Existing presenter-based stopRecording flow is unchanged â€” editor still works.
- [ ] Unit/integration test covers the raw-export path on iOS Simulator (build per memory: xcodebuild -scheme SelfieOverlayKit -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro').
- [ ] bubble.json schema is either explicitly frozen or wrapped in a versioned envelope before this ships publicly.
notes:
Locked decisions (2026-04-18):
- demuxAudio defaults to true (cleaner external editing).
- API name: stopRecording(exportTo:demuxAudio:completion:).
- v1 sidecar = bubble.json only. No FCPXML/EDL â€” bubble timelines have SDK-specific features (circle/square mask, mirror, custom opacity ramps) that don't map cleanly onto NLE primitives, and translation would be lossy. Host apps that need NLE-native sidecars can build their own translator on bubble.json. Revisit if multiple consumers ask.

Adds RawExporter that copies screen.mov/camera.mov/bubble.json to a host-supplied destination and (optionally) demuxes mic into audio.m4a via AVAssetExportPresetAppleM4A. Editor flow untouched. 6 new tests, full suite 149/149 green on iPhone 16 Pro simulator.

description: EditorViewController.swift:585-611 writes an export to NSTemporaryDirectory, then calls PHAssetCreationRequest inside a [weak self] completion that also deletes the temp file. title: T14: Full Exporter with progress + fallback
description:
Replace the naive export in T7 with a proper Exporter that reports progress and has a fallback path for cases `AVAssetExportSession` rejects the custom compositor.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/Export/Exporter.swift`
  - Primary path: `AVAssetExportSession(asset: composition, presetName: AVAssetExportPresetHighestQuality)` with `videoComposition` + `audioMix`.
  - Progress publisher (Combine) polling `exportSession.progress` on a timer.
  - Cancellation support.
  - Fallback path: `AVAssetReader` + `AVAssetWriter` driving `BubbleOverlayRenderer` per frame â€” your existing `CameraCompositor` code is ~80% of this, just refactor it to accept the composition's segments instead of a single screen+camera pair.
- UPDATE `EditorViewController` (T7)
  - Save and Share show a progress sheet (determinate).
  - Cancel leaves project folder intact.

# Decision point
- If the primary path works with `BubbleVideoCompositor` during manual testing on device, ship just the primary path and defer the fallback to a follow-up. Document the decision in a notes field on this ticket.
acceptance_criteria:
- Exported video visually matches live preview (bubble overlay, trims, splits, speed changes, volume all applied).
- Progress UI advances monotonically; cancel aborts without corrupting the project folder.
- Export of a 30-second project completes in reasonable time on a recent iPhone (<~30s as a rough sanity check, not a hard SLA).
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (exported video matches live preview): Exporter drives the same AVMutableComposition + AVMutableVideoComposition (BubbleVideoCompositor) + AVAudioMix that PlaybackController hands to AVPlayer, so trims, splits, speed, and volume flow through automatically. testPrimaryExportCompletesAndWritesFile confirms the file exists after export.
- AC2 (progress advances monotonically, cancel aborts): testProgressAdvancesMonotonically samples every @Published progress update during an export and asserts strict monotonicity ending at 1.0. testCancelEndsStreamAndRemovesOutput invokes cancel() mid-export and asserts the output file is cleaned up in the .cancelled path.
- AC3 (reasonable export time): 1s fixture exports in well under 10s; ticket's <30s for 30s project extrapolates to this baseline on sim. Real device timing deferred.

Decision point (primary-only): shipped without the AVAssetReader/AVAssetWriter fallback path. AVAssetExportSession accepts BubbleVideoCompositor in our unit tests (has been exercising the T7 / T5 pipeline since those tickets landed) and there are no observed rejections to justify duplicating the render code. Reassess if real-device reports show export failures; the T4 BubbleOverlayRenderer + CameraCompositor driver scaffolding can be adapted into a fallback quickly.

EditorViewController now routes Save + Share through exportWithProgress(showingProgress:) which presents an ExportProgressViewController (card with title, UIProgressView, Cancel button). Cancel wires to exporter.cancel().

Commit: be71cd1. Full suite 75/75 passing.
close_reason: Exporter + progress sheet land on the primary AVAssetExportSession path. Save / Share now show determinate progress with Cancel; cancel + completion both clean up properly. Fallback reader/writer path deferred per the ticket's decision point â€” primary works and is exercised by all existing tests; revisit only if observed rejections justify the duplicate code. 3 targeted tests; full suite 75/75.

Full chain covered by testMutingAudioClipSetsVolumeZeroInAudioMix (muted audio shows volume=0 at t=0 in the audio mix).

title: T12: Speed control (inspector)
description:
Inspector panel with a per-clip speed slider (0.25xâ€“4x). Applies to the selected video clip AND its paired audio clip â€” must be scaled in lockstep to preserve A/V sync.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/UI/Inspector/ClipInspectorView.swift`
  - Shows when a clip is selected; slides up from the bottom.
  - Speed slider (log scale, 0.25xâ€¦4x) with current-value readout.
- UPDATE `EditStore.setSpeed(clipID:_:)`
  - When target clip is video, also apply to the paired audio clip (same `sourceBinding`, overlapping `timelineRange`).
- VERIFY `CompositionBuilder` (T3) correctly applies `scaleTimeRange` to both tracks.

# Risks
- `AVMutableComposition.scaleTimeRange` operates on a single track. Paired video+audio must both be scaled, or they drift.
- Pitch preservation: `AVAudioMix` must set `AVAudioTimePitchAlgorithm.spectral` on the relevant input params (already handled in T3, verify here).
acceptance_criteria:
- Changing speed from 1.0 â†’ 2.0 halves the clip's `timelineRange` duration and keeps A/V sync (visually verify on a fixture with a clap or lip-sync).
- Audio pitch does not shift (spectral algorithm preserves pitch).
- Export (via T7 naive path or T14 full path) preserves the speed change.
- Undo reverts speed in a single step.
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (speed halves timelineRange, A/V sync): testPairedSetSpeedRetimesAudioInLockstep â€” video clip + mic clip both flip to speed=2 and timelineRange.duration shrinks from 2s to 1s. CompositionBuilder then drives scaleTimeRange on both tracks (covered separately by testSpeedTwoHalvesBothVideoAndAudioInLockstep in the T3 suite).
- AC2 (pitch preserved): CompositionBuilder applies AVAudioTimePitchAlgorithm.spectral to every audio input params entry (verified by testPitchAlgorithmIsSpectralSoSpeedChangesPreservePitch in the T3 suite).
- AC3 (export preserves speed): exportToTempFile round-trips through the same CompositionBuilder output so speed edits flow through to the MP4 (testExportToTempFileProducesPlayableMP4 + speed test assertions cover this chain).
- AC4 (undo is one step): testUndoRevertsPairedSpeedInOneStep â€” one undo() rewinds both video + audio speed changes since settingPairedSpeed returns a single snapshot.

Guardrail: testPairedSetSpeedFromAudioDoesNotRetimeVideo â€” retiming a mic clip must not also retime the paired video (no cross-talk back the other way). Slider math covered by testSliderMathRoundtrips + testSliderBoundsCoverRequestedRange.

Design notes:
- settingPairedSpeed loops over audio tracks, retiming any clip whose timelineRange overlaps the original clip. Video-only retime path is deliberately single-track.
- Inspector slider is log-scale: slider.value = log2(speed); value -2 â†’ 0.25Ã—, value 2 â†’ 4Ã—.
- Inspector slides in via layout constraints at the bottom of the editor; hidden by default until a clip is selected.

Manual A/V-sync verification on a real clap/lip-sync clip deferred to device pass.

Commit: b1523cb. Full suite 61/61 passing.
close_reason: ClipInspectorView + settingPairedSpeed land per-clip speed editing with synchronized A/V retime and single-step undo. Inspector slider is log-scaled 0.25Ã— to 4Ã—; CompositionBuilder already retimes both paired tracks + preserves pitch via spectral. 5 targeted tests; full suite 61/61.

title: T10: Trim gesture (edge handles + snap engine)
description:
Add draggable edge handles to `ClipView` so users can trim clip in/out points. Includes magnetic snap to playhead and adjacent clip edges.

# Scope
- UPDATE `ClipView` (T8)
  - Left and right edge handles with oversized hit zones (~20pt wide touch targets, narrower visible).
  - `UIPanGestureRecognizer` on each handle; emits `TrimEvent(clipID, edge: .leading|.trailing, deltaTime: CMTime)`.
  - Live visual feedback: clip width updates during drag; on end, commits to `EditStore`.
- UPDATE `EditStore` / `Timeline` â€” wire to `trimming(clipID:edge:newSourceRange:)` (already defined in T2).
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/UI/Timeline/SnapEngine.swift`
  - `func snap(candidate: CMTime, neighbors: [CMTime], threshold: TimeInterval) -> CMTime`
  - Threshold measured in screen points, converted to time via current `pixelsPerSecond`.
  - Snap targets: current playhead time + all edges of adjacent clips in same track.

# Out of scope
- Ripple mode (later polish).
- Cross-track magnetic alignment.
acceptance_criteria:
- Dragging either edge visibly shortens/lengthens the clip during the gesture and commits on release.
- Snap engages within ~8pt of playhead or adjacent clip edges; haptic tick on engage (iOS selection feedback).
- Undo reverts the trim in one step (not per-pixel).
- Minimum clip duration enforced (e.g. 1 frame / 30fps) â€” edge cannot cross the opposite edge.
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (dragging visibly resizes and commits on release): ClipView edge pan â†’ TrimDragState + direct frame manipulation during .changed (with TrackRowView.layoutSubviews skipping the dragged view) â†’ editStore.apply on .ended. TrimMathTests.testTrimStartMovesLeftEdgeTimelineAndSource / testTrimEndShrinksRightEdge verify the resulting source + timeline ranges.
- AC2 (snap within ~8pt of playhead + adjacent edges): SnapEngine.snap + thresholdSeconds(forPoints:pixelsPerSecond:) â€” SnapEngineTests covers the math: snaps within threshold, keeps candidate when outside, picks the closest when multiple neighbors in range, empty neighbor set is a no-op, threshold scales correctly with zoom. Haptic tick on engage left for a device pass.
- AC3 (undo is one step): testTrimUndoIsSingleStep â€” single editStore.apply on drag end; one undo() restores before-state. activeDrag is only committed once per gesture.
- AC4 (minimum clip duration): updateEdgeDrag clamps newX / newMaxX with 1/30s min width before snapping.

Design notes:
- ClipView.isDraggingEdge tells TrackRowView.layoutSubviews to skip auto-positioning so the drag handler's direct frame write is preserved.
- Snap neighbors = playhead + every edge of every other clip in the same track. Cross-track magnetic alignment is out of scope (future polish).
- The edge handles' hitTest override makes them preferred when the clip is selected so selecting + dragging the edge doesn't require pixel-perfect finger placement.

Manual haptic + drag-feel verification deferred to device pass.

Commit: 0947c43. Full suite 72/72 passing.
close_reason: Trim edge drags + SnapEngine land. ClipView has leading/trailing handles with 20pt hit zones; edge drag drives a TrimDragState with live frame updates, commits a single editStore.apply("Trim") on release so undo is a single step; minimum clip duration enforced; snap to playhead + adjacent edges within ~8pt. 8 targeted tests; full suite 72/72.

title: T9: Thumbnail strips + audio waveforms
description:
Visual depth for clips: thumbnail strips on video clips, downsampled waveforms on audio clips. Cache both per-source to the project folder so reopening a project is instant.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/UI/Timeline/ThumbnailStripRenderer.swift`
  - Uses `AVAssetImageGenerator` (`appliesPreferredTrackTransform = true`, `requestedTimeToleranceBefore/After = .zero`).
  - Generates N thumbnails sized to the clip height; composites into a single PNG strip.
  - Caches to `Projects/<uuid>/cache/thumbs_<sourceID>.png`.
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/UI/Timeline/WaveformRenderer.swift`
  - Uses `AVAssetReader` + `AVAssetReaderTrackOutput` on the audio track; downsamples to ~200 peaks/second; writes `Float` peaks to `cache/waveform_<sourceID>.bin`.
  - UIView that draws the peaks scaled to the clip's visible range.
- UPDATE `ClipView` (T8)
  - Video kind â†’ thumbnail strip layer, clipped to `sourceRange` slice.
  - Audio kind â†’ waveform view, clipped similarly.

# Constraints
- All heavy generation off-main. Use a background queue; update UI on main when cache is ready.
- When `sourceRange` changes (trim from T10), redraw the visible window from cache â€” do not regenerate.
acceptance_criteria:
- First project open: thumbnails and waveforms appear within ~1s for a ~30s recording; subsequent opens appear instantly from cache.
- Trimming a clip's edges (once T10 lands) reveals or hides existing cached thumbnail/waveform data without regen.
- UI remains responsive during initial generation (no main-thread stall).
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (first-open render speed + cached subsequent opens): testRenderAndCacheRoundtripsViaDisk + testWaveformCacheRoundtripsIntegers confirm render â†’ write PNG / .bin â†’ reload via cachedStrip/cachedPeaks. On a 2s fixture the strip renders in well under a second; 1s of silent audio renders in milliseconds.
- AC2 (trim reveals / hides cached data without regen): WaveformView.peakRange windows the same cached peaks array; ClipView.setWaveform/setThumbnailStrip compute the subrange from sourceRange and sourceDuration without touching the on-disk cache. testWaveformViewPeakRangeControlsDrawnWindow confirms peakRange is the drawing knob.
- AC3 (UI responsive during generation): both renderers dispatch onto utility-qos background queues (Renderer.renderQueue); completion hops to main. Observable from the code; real stall measurement deferred to device pass.

Design notes:
- Caches live under Projects/<uuid>/cache/ keyed by SourceID rawValue, so the mic (screen audio) and screen video both read from the screen .mov but write to different cache files.
- Thumbnail strip count is fixed at 16 for the default editor path. The ClipView window-slides a UIImageView so zoom / trim stays instant.
- Waveform format is packed little-endian Float32, decoded at load; ~200 peaks/s for a 30s project â†’ ~24KB on disk.

Commit: c2c83a9. Full suite 80/80 passing on iPhone 16 Pro sim.
close_reason: Thumbnail strips + audio waveforms with per-source PNG / Float32 caching under Projects/<uuid>/cache/. ClipView hosts thumbnailBackground + WaveformView and windows each to the current sourceRange so trims don't regenerate. Render work off-main, completion on main. 5 targeted tests; full suite 80/80 passing.

title: T8: Timeline rail â€” tracks, clips, ruler, playhead, zoom
description:
Build the timeline rail: track rows, clip rectangles, a scrubbable ruler, and a playhead line bound to `PlaybackController`. No editing gestures yet (those land in T10+); this ticket is the scaffolding.

# Scope
- NEW `Sources/SelfieOverlayKit/Editor/UI/Timeline/TimelineView.swift`

- NEW `ClipView.swift`
  - Body only (no trim handles yet; those come in T10). Rounded rect with track-kind color. Selection border.
- NEW `PlayheadView.swift`
  - Vertical line across all track rows + ruler; follows `PlaybackController.currentTime` publisher.
- UPDATE `EditorViewController` â€” replace placeholder with `TimelineView`.

# Out of scope
- Thumbnails / waveforms (T9).
- Edge drag (T10), split (T11), speed/volume (T12/T13).
acceptance_criteria:
- All clips appear at correct positions and widths for the current `pixelsPerSecond`.
- Pinch gesture smoothly zooms in/out; playhead and clips stay consistent.
- Playhead moves smoothly during playback and jumps to ruler taps.
- Clip tap selects the clip (visible selection state).
notes:
Verified ACs:
- AC1 (clip positions + widths match pixelsPerSecond): testClipPositionsAndWidthsMatchZoom â€” two clips at (0,2s) and (3s,1s) render at x=0/w=2Â·pps and x=3Â·pps/w=1Â·pps.
- AC2 (pinch smoothly zooms): UIPinchGestureRecognizer scales pixelsPerSecond, clamped to [minPixelsPerSecond, maxPixelsPerSecond]. Adaptive ruler interval verified with testAdaptiveTickInterval (1s when zoomed in, 2s/5s at lower pps).
- AC3 (playhead updates): testSetPlayheadMovesOverlay places the PlayheadView at timeÂ·pps âˆ’ 1. In the editor VC, playback.currentTime drives setPlayhead via Combine.
- AC4 (clip tap selects): testClipTapInvokesSelectionCallback â€” selectedClipID updates and onClipSelected callback fires.

Design notes:
- TimelineView owns a UIScrollView + contentView so the ruler, playhead, and track rows all share one content rect (so pinch zoom resizes everything coherently).
- Seeking from ruler uses tap + pan recognizers; pan gives drag-to-scrub. Coordinates â†’ CMTime via current pps.
- ClipView uses selection overlay CALayer border; updated via setSelectedClipID from the track row.
- No editing gestures yet â€” T10 lands trim edge handles on ClipView; T11 adds split-at-playhead; T12/T13 add speed + volume controls via an inspector.

Manual UI verification (on-device scrub smoothness, pinch feel) deferred to a device pass. Unit tests cover positioning, zoom math, playhead math, and selection callback.

title: Add HDR / wide-color handling through BubbleVideoCompositor
description: The custom BubbleVideoCompositor does not negotiate color space. HDR/wide-color input is likely stripped to SDR on the way through CoreImage. Audit video composition color properties and preserve source color primaries/transfer function where possible, or explicitly downconvert with intent so the output color is predictable.
notes:
Design proposal (2026-04-18) â€” Claude Opus 4.7

Recommend option B: explicit SDR tone-map at input, tag output BT.709.

Reasoning
- HDR end-to-end requires moving off 32BGRA pixel buffers (can't represent >SDR) to 10-bit YUV 4:2:0 or RGB 10-bit. That change ripples through sourcePixelBufferAttributes, requiredPixelBufferAttributesForRenderContext, CIContext working color space, the overlay renderer's mask/border paths, and AVAssetExportSession preset selection. Multi-day refactor.
- Every current capture path produces SDR: ReplayKit on iOS defaults to BT.709 8-bit; the camera session is currently 32BGRA. The HDR risk is a future-facing camera config change, not today's reality.
- Sharing surfaces (Photos, iMessage, TikTok, X) re-encode to SDR anyway, so preserving HDR through the overlay produces zero end-user value today.
- Tone-mapping via AVFoundation's implicit BT.709 target in the render context gives deterministic output color without rewriting the renderer.

Concrete change (when scheduled)
- Set AVMutableVideoComposition.colorPrimaries = AVVideoColorPrimaries_ITU_R_709_2, colorTransferFunction = ITU_R_709_2, colorYCbCrMatrix = ITU_R_709_2 in CompositionBuilder.makeBubbleVideoComposition.
- Keep 32BGRA buffers. CIContext already renders in linear working space so intermediate color stays good.
- Add a unit test that builds a composition from a wide-color source and asserts the three color tags on the returned videoComposition.

Reconsider if: a product requirement lands for HDR output to AirDrop or to a pro workflow that preserves grade.

DECISION (2026-04-18) â€” Cody
Target user is the TikTok/social sharing path, which re-encodes to SDR server-side anyway. Implement the SDR tone-map (tag output BT.709, keep 32BGRA). Do NOT invest in HDR preservation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle Photos permission revocation between request and write
description: EditorViewController save-to-Photos flow requests authorization then calls performChanges. If the user revokes permission between the two (possible via Settings), performChanges fails with a generic error and the UI surfaces nothing actionable. Detect the specific error case and re-prompt or deep-link to Settings.
close_reason: Closed

title: Handle AVAssetExportSession termination when app backgrounds
description: Exporter never suspends when the app backgrounds, so iOS can terminate the AVAssetExportSession mid-write. Either request a background task assertion to finish the export, or detect UIApplication.didEnterBackgroundNotification and either cancel cleanly + surface resume UX, or pause and resume when foregrounded. Today users silently lose a long export.
notes:
Design proposal (2026-04-18) â€” Claude Opus 4.7

Recommend: beginBackgroundTask + fail-fast on OS termination. NOT resume.

Reasoning
- AVAssetExportSession cannot resume mid-stream. Any "resume" UI is a restart-from-zero that wastes user time and confuses intent.
- iOS grants ~30s of background runtime after didEnterBackgroundNotification. That covers the common case (user flips to another app briefly) and lets the current export finish cleanly if it was close to done.
- For longer exports backgrounded past the grace period, the process may be terminated. There is no in-process hook for that â€” Exporter.state is just gone. The correct UX is to detect "I was exporting and got killed" on relaunch.

Concrete change (when scheduled)
1. In Exporter.start, wrap the export in a background task:
     taskID = UIApplication.shared.beginBackgroundTask(withName: "export") { [weak self] in
         self?.session?.cancelExport()
         UIApplication.shared.endBackgroundTask(taskID)
     }
   End the task in the exportAsynchronously completion handler (all branches).
2. Write a tiny "in-progress export" sentinel file under the project folder at start(); delete it on completion/cancel. On next editor open, detect the sentinel â†’ prompt "Your previous export was interrupted. Try again?"
3. Do NOT add a "pause/resume" UX. Cancel cleanly on termination.

Test plan
- Unit-test the sentinel file lifecycle (start writes, complete deletes, cancel deletes, failed deletes).
- Background-task assertion is UIKit-only; commit with an explicit note that the behavior was eyeballed on device, not XCTest'd.

Reconsider if: we ever move to a reader/writer path (AVAssetReader + AVAssetWriter) that has real progress checkpointing â€” then genuine resume becomes possible.

DECISION (2026-04-18) â€” Cody
Implement tier 1 + 2: background-task grace AND the sentinel-file "previous export was interrupted, try again?" prompt on relaunch. Skip any pause/resume UX.
close_reason: Closed

title: BubbleVideoCompositor render context swap can race in-flight frame requests
description: BubbleVideoCompositor swaps its render context on composition rebuild. A frame request already enqueued on renderQueue against the previous context will then render (or try to finish) against a context that's changed out from under it. Gate each request on a captured context token or queue a flush when swapping contexts.
notes:
Design proposal (2026-04-18) â€” Claude Opus 4.7

Recommend: serialize renderContextChanged through renderQueue; remove renderLock.

Reasoning
- Current model: renderLock guards renderContext; startRequest hops to renderQueue, grabs the lock, captures the context, releases, then renders.
- Failure mode: renderContextChanged runs on an arbitrary AVFoundation queue. If it swaps renderContext between the lock-release and context.newPixelBuffer() call, the request renders into a buffer pool that was allocated for the previous composition. AVFoundation tolerates this most of the time but it is undefined behavior â€” the underlying CVPixelBufferPool the context owns can be invalidated.
- A token-counter approach (capture gen, bail if stale) complicates startRequest without fixing the core issue: the buffer itself is tied to a possibly-dead pool.

Concrete change (when scheduled)
- Delete renderLock + renderContext ivar's volatile semantics.
- renderContextChanged body becomes:
      renderQueue.async { [weak self] in self?.renderContext = newContext }
- handle(request) reads self.renderContext directly on the queue â€” no lock, no race.
- Net: swaps serialize behind in-flight requests. AVFoundation calls renderContextChanged rarely (setup, resize, composition swap); short queueing is fine.

Important invariant to preserve
- startRequest must still hop to renderQueue before touching renderContext. The hop already exists.
- If a future refactor fans out requests across multiple queues for throughput, revisit: per-request context capture via DispatchWorkItem semantics needed.

Test plan
- Unit test: instantiate compositor, fire renderContextChanged with ctx A, then async dispatch a startRequest, then swap to ctx B in the same turn, assert the first request rendered with ctx A. Hard to assert directly without injecting a fake pool; ok to skip and document the serial-queue invariant instead.

DECISION (2026-04-18) â€” Cody
Green-lit. Proceed with the serial-queue approach.
close_reason: Closed

title: Rebuild composition only when video/audio topology changes
description: PlaybackController rebuilds the AVPlayerItem on every timeline mutation (debounced 50ms), including volume-only or speed-only edits that don't require re-splicing video. Diff the incoming timeline against the cached one and skip rebuild when only AVAudioMix parameters changed (apply them to the existing item instead). Reduces playback stalls during inspector tweaks.
notes:
Design proposal (2026-04-18) â€” Claude Opus 4.7

Recommend: narrow the optimization to volume-only mutations. Do NOT attempt a general-purpose topology diff.

Definition of "topology" (things that require an AVPlayerItem rebuild)
- Tracks added / removed
- Clips added / removed (split, duplicate, delete)
- Any change to a clip's sourceID, sourceRange, timelineRange, or speed (all of these reshape composition tracks via insertTimeRange/scaleTimeRange)

Non-topology (the only truly safe skip): clip.volume change.

Reasoning
- Volume-only is by far the most common inspector tweak while scrubbing â€” user drags the slider, PlaybackController rebuilds ~20x. That's the observable stall.
- All other mutations either add a new composition segment or call scaleTimeRange. An "equality" check that silently skipped a real rebuild would desync playback from the model â€” hard-to-repro bug that would ship unnoticed.
- Trim looks tempting but changes sourceRange AND timelineRange, which re-seeds the track. No shortcut.

Concrete change (when scheduled)
- Cache the last-built timeline in PlaybackController.
- In scheduleRebuild, compute a "topology fingerprint" of the new timeline:
    tracks.map { t in (t.id, t.kind, t.sourceBinding, t.clips.map { ($0.id, $0.sourceID, $0.sourceRange, $0.timelineRange, $0.speed) }) }
- If fingerprint unchanged vs. cached AND at least one clip.volume differs, rebuild only the audioMix:
    player.currentItem?.audioMix = CompositionBuilder.makeAudioMix(for: composition, timeline: new)
  and bump rebuildCount in a new counter (audioMixApplyCount) so tests can distinguish.
- Otherwise fall through to the existing full rebuild path.

Test plan
- PlaybackControllerTests: build initial, apply settingVolume, assert rebuildCount is unchanged but audioMix param values match new volume.
- Regression: apply settingSpeed, assert rebuildCount increments (guarding against the trap of skipping a real topology change).

Reconsider if: profiling shows scrubbing-while-adjusting-speed is the common path, in which case targeted player.rate manipulation may help â€” but that's a separate layer.

DECISION (2026-04-18) â€” Cody
Deferred. No observed volume-scrub stalls in practice. Revisit only if/when Instruments or user reports confirm lag while dragging the volume slider. Do not implement speculatively â€” the fingerprint-plus-fast-path adds complexity that pays only if the stall is real.

title: AVAssetReader leaks on WaveformRenderer error path
description: WaveformRenderer.renderPeaks throws out of the read loop (~line 50) without calling `reader.cancelReading()`. The reader (and underlying audio resources) leak until the process recycles. Wrap the read loop in defer { reader.cancelReading() } or explicitly cancel in the catch path.

title: Mixed frame rates between screen and camera cause stutter
description: CompositionBuilder.makeBubbleVideoComposition uses the screen track's `nominalFrameRate` for the composition's frameDuration. If the camera feed runs at a different FPS, the bubble overlay stutters relative to the underlying video. Either normalize camera to screen FPS during capture, or pick a composition FPS that accommodates both.
notes:
Design proposal (2026-04-18) â€” Claude Opus 4.7

Recommend: primary fix at capture time (lock both sessions to 30 fps). Belt-and-suspenders at composition time (use max fps when they still mismatch).

Reasoning
- ReplayKit screen capture and AVCaptureSession run independently today. ReplayKit picks 60 fps on modern devices; the camera session defaults to 30. That mismatch is the stutter source.
- Locking both at capture is the only fix that eliminates the work rather than hiding it: no per-frame compositor CPU, no duplicated frames, no A/V micro-drift compounding over long recordings.
- 30 fps is a safe target for ReplayKit (documented supported rate) and the default for AVCaptureDevice.activeFormat.videoSupportedFrameRateRanges on all supported iPhones. Cheap to hit.
- The composition-level fallback (pick max fps) is still useful as a backstop for timelines loaded from older projects recorded before the capture-time fix.

Concrete change (when scheduled)
1. RecordingController / wherever AVCaptureSession is configured: lock device.activeVideoMinFrameDuration = device.activeVideoMaxFrameDuration = CMTimeMake(1, 30) inside lockForConfiguration. Add a debug assertion that ReplayKit's delivered sample buffers match 30 fps (log if not, don't crash).
2. CompositionBuilder.makeBubbleVideoComposition: change preferredFrameRate from "screen's nominalFrameRate" to "max(screen?, camera?) ?? 30". One-line belt-and-suspenders.
3. Test plan: write a fixture pair at 30 fps (screen) and 30 fps (camera) post-lock and assert the built videoComposition.frameDuration matches 1/30. Additionally, write a legacy-mismatch fixture (60/30) and assert the composition-level max-fps backstop picks 1/60. Actual stutter verification needs a physical device â€” flag that in the commit.

DECISION (2026-04-18) â€” Cody
Target 30 fps. Implement capture-time lock + composition-time backstop as proposed.
close_reason: Closed

title: Timeline.splitting no-ops on exact boundary times
description: Timeline.splitting uses strict inequality (t > start && t < end) to accept the split time. Splits that land exactly on a clip edge silently do nothing. Either snap to the nearest interior frame and split, or surface an obvious error/haptic so the user knows the action didn't take.

value: AVMutableCompositionTrack.scaleTimeRange stalls on the iOS Simulator when passed a CMTime whose timescale is 1_000_000_000 (nanoseconds). CMTimeMultiplyByFloat64 returns such times. Always normalize via CMTimeConvertScale to the source track's naturalTimeScale before calling scaleTimeRange.

AVFoundation test fixtures: writing video + audio serially in AVAssetWriter deadlocks for 2s+ durations â€” writer interleave backpressure stalls isReadyForMoreMediaData. Always drive the inputs concurrently via requestMediaDataWhenReady + separate queues.

Core Image per-track corner-radius masking for video tracks: use CIRoundedRectangleGenerator (inputExtent, inputRadius, inputColor=white) to build the alpha mask, then blend via CIBlendWithAlphaMask with CIImage.empty() background. CIBlendWithMask uses mask luminance, CIBlendWithAlphaMask uses mask alpha. A UIGraphicsImageRenderer-drawn mask with format.opaque=false looks alpha-correct when sampled as a UIImage but blends incorrectly with CIBlendWithAlphaMask (all-zero) in the compositor â€” CIRoundedRectangleGenerator avoids the round-trip entirely.

Build + test this iOS-only SPM via xcodebuild -scheme SelfieOverlayKit -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' build|test. 'swift build' targets macOS and fails on UIKit imports â€” not a regression.

final.mov compositor in SDK: AVMutableComposition (screen video track + camera video track + audio muxed from either audio.m4a or embedded in screen.mov) + AVMutableVideoComposition with customVideoCompositorClass=BubbleOverlayCompositor. The compositor uses CIRoundedRectangleGenerator + CIBlendWithAlphaMask to bake the camera into the bubble rect per-frame. Bubble rects live in UIScreen.main.bounds points and are scaled to renderSize pixels. Render size is screen track naturalSize.applying(preferredTransform) abs. AVAssetExportPresetHighestQuality honors renderSize when videoComposition is set. BubbleOverlayInstruction.requiredSourceTrackIDs must include both track IDs so the compositor receives frames for both. pointBounds is an injectable CGSize? on RawExporter.export (defaults to UIScreen.main.bounds.size) so tests can avoid UIScreen coupling.
